tool.name: Tool

param.name: input
param.dialogReference: Select your input DTED or GeoTIFF file, or a folder containing multiple files.
param.pythonReference: `-i`, `--input`: Path to input file or directory

param.name: output
param.dialogReference: Specify the output location for the transformed file(s).
param.pythonReference: `-o`, `--output`: Path for output file or directory

param.name: source_datum
param.dialogReference: Select the source vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008).
param.pythonReference: `-s`, `--source_datum`: Source vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008)

param.name: target_datum
param.dialogReference: Select the target vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008).
param.pythonReference: `-t`, `--target_datum`: Target vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008)

param.name: algorithm
param.expression: {Bilinear Interpolation | Thin Plate Spline | Delaunay Triangulation}
param.dialogReference: Choose the interpolation method (Bilinear Interpolation, Thin Plate Spline, Delaunay Triangulation); defaults to Bilinear Interpolation.
param.pythonReference: `-a`, `--algorithm`: Interpolation algorithm to use (optional; choices: 'bilinear', 'spline', 'delaunay'; default: 'bilinear')

param.name: min_patch_size
param.dialogReference: Specify the minimum size (in pixels) for flat areas to be retained.
param.pythonReference: `-p`, `--min_patch_size`: Minimum size in pixels for a flat area to be retained (optional; default: 16)

param.name: abs_horiz_accuracy
param.displayname: Absolute Horizontal Accuracy (applied only if missing)
param.dialogReference: Specify the absolute horizontal accuracy in meters. This value is only applied to DTED files, and only if it is missing ('NA') in the source file.
param.pythonReference: `-h`, `--abs_horiz_accuracy`: Absolute horizontal accuracy in meters (for DTED output only, if missing)

param.name: flatten
param.dialogReference: Check this box to preserve flat areas and keep ocean at 0 during transformation.
param.pythonReference: `-f`, `--flatten`: Whether to retain flat areas (optional; default: True)

param.name: create_mask
param.dialogReference: Check this box to create a mask of ocean and other flat areas.
param.pythonReference: `-m`, `--create_mask`: Whether to create a flat mask file (optional, default: False)

tool.summary: This toolbox provides a comprehensive solution for transforming vertical datums between the WGS 84 ellipsoid and the EGM96 and EGM2008 geoids for DTED and GeoTIFF files. The transformation tool uses the bilinear interpolation algorithm to resample NGA's global geoid undulation models at one arc second (~1.8 km) resolution to the input DEM resolution, then applies the difference to generate the output DEM. An EGMTrans Explorer map, available for both ArcGIS Pro and QGIS, stores the full-resolution geoid models and allows users to interrogate datum transformations performed by this tool or other software and identify datum errors.
tool.usage:
• Performs transformations between WGS84, EGM96, and EGM2008 vertical datums

  • Handles both DTED (Digital Terrain Elevation Data) and GeoTIFF file formats

  • Processes either individual files or entire directories

  • Applies scale factors and offsets automatically when needed

  • Outputs Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) format for non-DTED results

  • Preserves ocean and flat areas during transformation with customizable patch size

  • Creates optional mask files of ocean and other flat regions for further analysis

  • Utilizes parallel processing for 20-50x performance improvement if Python's Numba library is available

  • Supports NGA's most widely used coordinate reference systems, including geographic, UTM and polar stereographic projections

  • Permits visualization and comparison of the geoids with each other and elevation datasets with the EGMTrans Explorer in ArcGIS and QGIS formats

scriptExample.title: EGMTrans Tool
scriptExample.para:
The basic syntax for using EGMTrans in a terminal or command prompt is:

`python EGMTrans.py -i INPUT -o OUTPUT -s SOURCE_DATUM -t TARGET_DATUM [-f FLATTEN] [-m CREATE_MASK] [-p MIN_PATCH_SIZE] [-a ALGORITHM]`

Arguments:

- `-i`, `--input`: Path to input file or directory

- `-o`, `--output`: Path for output file or directory

- `-s`, `--source_datum`: Source vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008)

- `-t`, `--target_datum`: Target vertical datum (WGS84, EGM96, or EGM2008)

- `-f`, `--flatten`: Whether to retain flat areas (optional; default: True)

- `-m`, `--create_mask`: Whether to create a flat mask file (optional, default: False)

- `-p`, `--min_patch_size`: Minimum size in pixels for a flat area to be retained (optional; default: 16)

- `-a`, `--algorithm`: Interpolation algorithm to use (optional; choices: 'bilinear', 'delaunay', 'spline', 'proj'; default: 'bilinear')Transform a Copernicus DEM from EGM2008 to EGM96, retaining flat areas:

dataIdInfo.idCredit: National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) Office of Geomatics, Elevation Division 3838 Vogel Road Mail Stop L-041 Arnold, MO 63010 +1 314-676-9146 terrain@nga.mil
